# UC-22.10.30 · Media Controls — Large PHI Print Jobs to Non-Secure Printers

## Description

Monitors unusually large chart print jobs routed to non-secure print queues so physical disclosure risks from uncontrolled hardcopy are reduced.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes windows and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Classify printers by site/zone; (2) integrate Pharos/Equitrac if used; (3) educate on minimum necessary printing. Indexes required: `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational`; Lookups: `secure_print_queues`, `secure_print_queues.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational` 307 (`Document`, `Pages`, `PrinterName`, `UserName`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Classify printers by site/zone; (2) integrate Pharos/Equitrac if used; (3) educate on minimum necessary printing.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational" EventCode="307" earliest=-7d
| eval pages=tonumber(Pages)
| lookup secure_print_queues.csv PrinterName OUTPUT secure_queue
| where (isnull(secure_queue) OR secure_queue="false") AND pages>=50
| stats sum(pages) as total_pages count as jobs by UserName, PrinterName
| sort - total_pages
```

Understanding this SPL

**Media Controls — Large PHI Print Jobs to Non-Secure Printers (§164.310(d)(2))** — Monitors unusually large chart print jobs routed to non-secure print queues so physical disclosure risks from uncontrolled hardcopy are reduced.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational` 307 (`Document`, `Pages`, `PrinterName`, `UserName`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=windows, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pages** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where (isnull(secure_queue) OR secure_queue="false") AND pages>=50` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by UserName, PrinterName** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (user, printer, pages), Bar chart (jobs by location).

## SPL

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational" EventCode="307" earliest=-7d
| eval pages=tonumber(Pages)
| lookup secure_print_queues.csv PrinterName OUTPUT secure_queue
| where (isnull(secure_queue) OR secure_queue="false") AND pages>=50
| stats sum(pages) as total_pages count as jobs by UserName, PrinterName
| sort - total_pages
```

## Visualization

Table (user, printer, pages), Bar chart (jobs by location).
